decisions.rationale: Frappe uses a generic 'exceeds available stock' phrasing. Odoo uses 'cannot allocate more than available quantity'. Afenda normalizes to line-level phrasing: 'Allocated quantity exceeds available stock for this line.' for precision in multi-line allocation contexts.
decisions.odooCandidate: You cannot allocate more than the available quantity.
decisions.resolvedValue: Allocated quantity exceeds available stock for this line.

decisions.rationale: Odoo uses 'Payments' for the settlement area. Afenda distinguishes 'Settlement' (reconciliation/netting) from 'Payment' (instrument/transaction) as separate business concepts in the ERP domain model.
Both Frappe and Odoo use bare 'Pending'. Afenda adds 'settlement' qualifier for clarity when displayed alongside payment-pending states.
Odoo uses 'Reconciled' which implies accounting reconciliation. Afenda uses 'Settled' to match the Settlement domain and avoid confusion with bank reconciliation.
Frappe uses 'Accounts', Odoo uses 'Invoicing'. Afenda uses 'Finance' as a broader term encompassing all financial management capabilities, not just invoicing or chart of accounts.
Odoo uses 'Contacts' (broader, includes vendors/suppliers). Afenda separates customer-facing CRM from vendor management; 'Customers' is the correct scoped label.
Odoo uses 'Reporting' (gerund form). Afenda uses 'Reports' (noun) consistent with the dashboard card naming pattern (Inventory, Sales, Customers, etc.).
Frappe uses 'Login', Odoo uses 'Log in'. Afenda uses 'ERP Login' as a branded entry point title that identifies the system context.
Both systems use bare 'Dashboard'. Afenda uses 'ERP Dashboard' for brand context in the application chrome.
Frappe uses 'Stock', Odoo uses 'Inventory'. Afenda uses 'Inventory Management' as the full page title to differentiate from the dashboard card label.
Frappe uses 'Selling', Odoo uses 'Sales'. Afenda uses 'Sales Management' as the full page title.
Frappe uses 'Accounting', Odoo uses 'Invoicing'. Afenda uses 'Financial Management' as the domain-neutral page title.
Frappe uses 'Human Resources', Odoo uses 'Employees'. Afenda uses 'Employee Management' as the full page title for consistency with the Management suffix pattern.
Both Frappe and Odoo use 'Settings'. Afenda uses 'System Settings' to differentiate from user-level preferences.

Frappe uses 'Stock Entry' (transaction-focused), Odoo uses 'Stock Move' (movement-focused). Afenda uses 'Allocations' to represent the business concept of reserving/committing inventory to specific orders, distinct from generic stock operations.
